project.name: markiniser
project.description: Local-first markdown organizer for browsing, searching, and editing markdown files on localhost.

nodes.name: markiniser.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses command-line arguments via Commander, bootstraps the core library with a resolved config, starts the server, and registers process-level signal handlers for graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates CLI startup: defines commands and options, loads config, initialises core, and starts the HTTP/WebSocket server.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and resolves the Markiniser configuration from disk using cosmiconfig, expanding tilde home paths and relative directory roots into absolute paths.

nodes.name: loadConfig
nodes.summary: Searches for a markiniser config file using cosmiconfig and returns a normalised MarkiniserConfig with resolved absolute paths.

nodes.name: fileAccess.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a sandboxed file-access factory that restricts read/write operations to a set of allowed root directories, preventing path-traversal and enforcing markdown-only constraints.

nodes.name: createFileAccess
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a FileAccessManager object with read, write, getMetadata, and validatePath methods scoped to the provided allowed root directories.

nodes.name: normalizeCandidatePath
nodes.summary: Resolves and validates that a candidate file path is a real, accessible path on the filesystem before granting access.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Public API barrel for the core package, re-exporting all major services and defining the top-level createCore factory that wires together scanning, indexing, file access, and watching.

nodes.name: createCore
nodes.summary: Top-level factory that initialises and wires the scanner, search indexer, file accessor, and file watcher into a single Core interface.

nodes.name: indexer.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a full-text search indexer over markdown files using FlexSearch, supporting add, remove, update, and ranked search with contextual snippet extraction.

nodes.name: createSearchIndexer
nodes.summary: Factory that builds a FlexSearch Document index from an initial file list and returns a SearchIndexer with add/remove/update/search methods.

nodes.name: createSnippet
nodes.summary: Extracts a short contextual snippet from a document body that surrounds the first match of the search query term.

nodes.name: search
nodes.summary: Queries the FlexSearch index across title and body fields and maps results to SearchResult objects with snippets, deduplicating by file path.

nodes.name: scanner.ts
nodes.summary: Recursively scans configured root directories for markdown files using fast-glob, reads YAML front-matter, and constructs both a flat file list and a nested directory tree.

nodes.name: scanMarkdownFiles
nodes.summary: Main scan entry point that globs markdown files from all configured roots, reads their front-matter and content, and assembles a ScanResult containing the flat file list and directory tree.

nodes.name: insertFileNode
nodes.summary: Recursively inserts a file into the in-memory directory tree by creating intermediate directory nodes as needed.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all shared TypeScript interfaces for the core package: MarkiniserConfig, FlatFile, TreeNode, SearchResult, and FileMetadata.

nodes.name: watcher.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a file-system watcher using chokidar that monitors markdown roots, emits typed events (add, change, remove) on the search indexer and scan result, and exposes a typed EventEmitter-based FileWatcher interface.

nodes.name: MarkiniserWatcher
nodes.summary: Core watcher class extending EventEmitter that owns a chokidar FSWatcher instance and coordinates file add/change/remove events with the scanner and search indexer.

nodes.name: createFileWatcher
nodes.summary: Thin factory function that constructs and returns a new MarkiniserWatcher instance.

nodes.name: config.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for loadConfig, covering default value merging, tilde home path expansion, and config file discovery via cosmiconfig in temp directories.

nodes.name: fileAccess.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for createFileAccess, verifying path-traversal prevention, markdown-only enforcement, allowed-root sandboxing, and read/write/metadata operations in temp directories.

nodes.name: indexer.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for the createSearchIndexer function, covering filename/path/content search, incremental index mutations (add, update, remove), and empty-query edge cases.

nodes.name: scanner.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest integration test suite for scanMarkdownFiles, using real temp directories to verify recursive discovery, exclusion patterns, file metadata correctness, and permission-denied handling.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Exports shared server-side constants including the localhost bind address, the frontend dist directory label, and a fallback HTML page shown when the frontend bundle is absent.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Fastify server factory that wires CORS (localhost-only), WebSocket support, REST routes, and static file serving into a single createServer function exported for use by the CLI entry point.

nodes.name: routes.ts
nodes.summary: Registers all Fastify REST API routes for the markdown organizer: GET /api/files (tree), GET/PUT /api/files/:path (read/write individual files), and GET /api/search (full-text search), with TypeBox schema validation and structured error mapping.

nodes.name: static.ts
nodes.summary: Registers @fastify/static to serve the pre-built frontend bundle, with a graceful fallback HTML page and a warning callback when the dist directory does not yet exist.

nodes.name: ws.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the WebSocket layer: registers the /ws endpoint, manages a live set of connected clients, and bridges core watcher events (file-added, file-changed, file-removed) to real-time JSON broadcasts.

nodes.name: server.test.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Vitest integration test suite for the Fastify server, testing all REST endpoints (file listing, file read/write, search) and WebSocket event broadcasting using real temp directories and a live server instance.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the web package that re-exports the App component and the AppStoreProvider/createAppStore store primitives for consumers of @markiniser/web.

nodes.name: registerRoutes
nodes.summary: Registers all REST API routes on the Fastify instance, covering the file tree, individual file read/write with index synchronisation, and full-text search with TypeBox schema validation.

nodes.name: mapFileAccessError
nodes.summary: Maps raw filesystem and domain errors to HTTP status codes (403 for disallowed roots, 400 for non-markdown paths, 404 for missing files, 500 for unexpected errors).

nodes.name: bindWatcherBroadcasts
nodes.summary: Subscribes to core watcher events and broadcasts JSON-serialised file-change messages to all open WebSocket clients; returns a cleanup function to detach listeners.

nodes.name: registerWebSocket
nodes.summary: Registers the @fastify/websocket plugin, manages the live socket set at the /ws endpoint, and wires watcher broadcasts with cleanup on server close.

nodes.name: createServer
nodes.summary: Factory function that assembles a fully configured Fastify server instance with CORS, WebSocket, REST routes, and static serving, accepting a Core instance and optional frontend dist path.

layers.name: CLI Entry Point
layers.description: Command-line bootstrap that parses arguments, resolves configuration, starts the Fastify server, and manages process lifecycle with graceful shutdown signals.

layers.name: Core Service Layer
layers.description: Domain services for markdown file scanning, full-text search indexing, sandboxed file access, configuration loading, filesystem watching, and shared TypeScript interfaces that form the business logic of the organizer.

layers.name: API Server Layer
layers.description: Fastify server wiring that exposes REST routes and a WebSocket broadcast channel for real-time file events, serves the pre-built frontend bundle as static assets, and defines shared server-side constants.

layers.name: Web Client Layer
layers.description: React frontend package entry point that re-exports the client application components and types consumed by the browser-based markdown browsing and editing UI.

layers.name: Test Suite Layer
layers.description: Vitest unit and integration test suites covering core services (config, file access, indexer, scanner) and the Fastify server HTTP and WebSocket endpoints.

tour.title: The CLI Bootstrap: Where It All Begins
tour.description: Every markiniser session starts at `bin/markiniser.js`. This is the executable that users run from their terminal. It uses Commander to parse flags, calls `loadConfig` to discover which markdown directories to watch, then wires up the core and server before registering OS signal handlers for clean shutdown. Reading this file answers the fundamental question: what actually happens when you type `markiniser`?
tour.languageLesson: The `main` function is an async IIFE pattern common in Node.js CLIs — it lets you use `await` at the top level and centralises all startup errors in one try/catch.

tour.title: Configuration Loading with cosmiconfig
tour.description: Before any files are scanned, markiniser needs to know which directories to look in. `packages/core/src/config.ts` handles this via cosmiconfig, which searches for config files in conventional locations (`.markiniserrc`, `markiniser.config.js`, etc.). The `loadConfig` function resolves all configured paths to absolute form, making the rest of the system location-independent. This is the first dependency the CLI bootstraps.
tour.languageLesson: cosmiconfig implements the XDG-style config file discovery pattern — it searches upward from the cwd through a prioritised list of filenames and formats, so users never have to specify where their config lives.

tour.title: The Shared Type Vocabulary
tour.description: With 8 fan-in edges, `packages/core/src/types.ts` is the most-imported file in the entire codebase — every core service depends on it. This file defines the TypeScript interfaces that give the whole system a shared language: what a `MarkdownFile` looks like, what a `ScanResult` contains, what a `SearchResult` carries. Understanding these shapes before diving into any service will make the rest of the code immediately legible.
tour.languageLesson: Centralising all domain interfaces in a single `types.ts` barrel is a deliberate architectural choice — it prevents circular imports between services and creates a single source of truth for data shapes.

tour.title: The Core Barrel: createCore Wires Everything
tour.description: `packages/core/src/index.ts` is the public API surface of the core package and the highest-scoring entry point in the graph. Its sole exported factory, `createCore`, takes a resolved config and returns a fully initialised `Core` object containing the scanner, search indexer, file accessor, and file watcher — all wired together. This is the composition root: understanding it shows you how every core service relates to every other.
tour.languageLesson: The factory-returns-interface pattern (`createCore` returns a typed `Core` object) avoids class-based DI while still making dependencies explicit and testable — callers depend on the interface, not the concrete implementations.

tour.title: Scanning the File System for Markdown
tour.description: `packages/core/src/scanner.ts` implements the discovery pass: it globs all configured root directories for `*.md` files using fast-glob and assembles a `ScanResult` containing a flat file list and an in-memory directory tree. The `insertFileNode` helper recursively builds the tree structure as files are discovered. This is the first thing `createCore` runs — you cannot search or watch what has not been found first.

tour.title: Full-Text Search with FlexSearch
tour.description: Once the scanner has a file list, `packages/core/src/indexer.ts` indexes every document for full-text search. `createSearchIndexer` builds a FlexSearch `Document` index seeded with the initial scan, then exposes `add`, `remove`, `update`, and `search` methods. The `search` function queries across both title and body fields, and `createSnippet` extracts a highlighted excerpt around the first match. This is what powers the search bar in the UI.
tour.languageLesson: FlexSearch's `Document` index maps field names to separate sub-indexes, letting you query title and body with different relevance weights — the `search` call merges and deduplicates results from both.

tour.title: Sandboxed File Access: Reading and Writing Safely
tour.description: `packages/core/src/fileAccess.ts` enforces a critical security boundary: no file read or write can escape the configured root directories. `createFileAccess` returns a `FileAccessManager` whose every method calls `normalizeCandidatePath` first — resolving symlinks and checking that the resolved path still falls within an allowed root. This prevents path-traversal attacks from a web client asking to read `../../etc/passwd`.
tour.languageLesson: `path.resolve` followed by a `startsWith` check on the allowed root is the canonical Node.js path-traversal mitigation — always resolve symlinks before comparing, since a symlink inside the root can point outside it.

tour.title: Live File Watching with MarkiniserWatcher
tour.description: `packages/core/src/watcher.ts` closes the real-time loop. The `MarkiniserWatcher` class extends `EventEmitter` and wraps chokidar to watch all configured markdown roots. When a file is added, changed, or deleted, it re-runs the relevant scanner and indexer operations and emits typed events that the server layer can subscribe to. `createFileWatcher` is the thin factory that `createCore` calls to instantiate it.
tour.languageLesson: Extending `EventEmitter` and emitting typed string events (`file:add`, `file:change`, `file:unlink`) is idiomatic Node.js pub/sub — it decouples the watcher from the server without requiring a shared state object.

tour.title: The Fastify Server: Wiring the HTTP Layer
tour.description: `packages/server/src/index.ts` is where the core services meet the network. The `createServer` factory accepts a `Core` instance, registers CORS, mounts the REST routes, sets up WebSocket broadcasting, and registers the static file handler for the pre-built frontend bundle. Every HTTP concern is delegated to a focused module — `routes.ts`, `ws.ts`, and `static.ts` — making the server composition easy to read at a glance.

tour.title: REST API Routes: Browsing, Reading, Writing, and Searching
tour.description: `packages/server/src/routes.ts` exposes the three core capabilities of the README — browse, search, and edit — as REST endpoints. `registerRoutes` adds routes for the file tree, file read/write (delegating to the sandboxed `FileAccessManager`), and full-text search (delegating to the `SearchIndexer`). The `mapFileAccessError` helper translates filesystem error codes into appropriate HTTP status codes so the client always gets meaningful responses.

tour.title: Real-Time Updates via WebSocket
tour.description: `packages/server/src/ws.ts` bridges the watcher events from the core layer to the browser. `registerWebSocket` sets up the `@fastify/websocket` plugin and maintains a live set of connected sockets. `bindWatcherBroadcasts` subscribes to `MarkiniserWatcher` events and fans each change out to every connected client as a JSON message. This is what allows the browser UI to reflect file-system changes without a page refresh.
tour.languageLesson: Maintaining a `Set<WebSocket>` and iterating it on every broadcast is the standard Fastify WebSocket fan-out pattern — the set is cleaned up in the `close` handler to prevent memory leaks from stale connections.

tour.title: Serving the Frontend Bundle
tour.description: `packages/server/src/static.ts` completes the server by registering `@fastify/static` to serve the pre-built React frontend from the path defined in `constants.ts`. This is what a browser receives when it navigates to `localhost` — the same Fastify process that answers API calls also delivers the UI, making the whole application a single `node` process with no separate dev server required in production.

tour.title: Test Coverage: Confidence at Every Layer
tour.description: The test suite uses Vitest across every layer of the stack. Unit tests for `config.ts` and `fileAccess.ts` verify config resolution and sandbox enforcement in isolation. Integration tests for `scanner.ts` and `indexer.ts` exercise the real filesystem and FlexSearch index. The server integration suite in `packages/server/test/server.test.ts` spins up a real Fastify instance and exercises REST and WebSocket endpoints end-to-end — giving high confidence that the complete request path works correctly.
tour.languageLesson: Vitest's compatibility with the Jest API means existing Jest knowledge transfers directly, while Vitest's native ESM support and faster cold-start make it a better fit for TypeScript monorepos.
